Global (All).title: US and Germany Rule Out Russian Oil Ban Over Ukraine
Global (All).summary: US and Germany rule out implementing a Russian oil ban, a move that has been considered to increase pressure on Russia in its war with Ukraine.

Global (All).title: Beijing Imposes Travel Bans to Contain Growing Covid-19 Outbreak
Global (All).summary: China's capital city restricts travel, citing a surge in new COVID-19 infections, as the city struggles to control the spread of the virus.

Global (All).title: Russia to Hold Large-Scale Military Drills in Western Border Regions
Global (All).summary: Russia prepares for massive military drills in the western regions of the country, sparking concerns about potential conflict with neighboring countries.

United States.title: Biden Administration Seeks to Boost U.S. Semiconductor Production
United States.summary: The Biden administration is working on a plan to provide incentives to boost U.S.-based production of semiconductors, following a chip shortage in 2021. The move aims to reduce dependence on Asian suppliers and enhance national security.

United States.title: House Republicans Introduce Bill to Ban 'BDS' on Government Contracts
United States.summary: House Republicans have introduced a bill aimed at banning the Boycott, Divestment and Sanctions (BDS) movement on U.S. government contracts, a move seen as a direct challenge to the Biden administration's stance.

United States.title: FBI Investigates Ransomware Attack on Florida County's IT System
United States.summary: The FBI is probing a ransomware attack that compromised the IT system of a Florida county's election office, sparking concerns over potential vulnerabilities in the U.S. electoral system.

South Korea.title: Protests against South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol escalates
South Korea.summary: Anti-government protests have intensified in South Korea after President Yoon Suk-yeol pushed to pass the 'economy-first' budget. Protesters fear this will widen the wealth gap and undermine social welfare measures. Police responded with tear gas and water cannons, leading to clashes with demonstrators.

South Korea.title: North Korea fires ballistic missiles amid tensions
South Korea.summary: In a surprise move, North Korea fired two suspected ballistic missiles into the Sea of Japan, prompting a military drill by South Korea and the United States. The incident raised concerns of a heightened risk of conflict.

South Korea.title: Corruption scandals hit South Korea's finance and politics
South Korea.summary: South Korean authorities have initiated investigations into high-profile corruption scandals involving finance executives and politicians. This scandal has already toppled several high-ranking officials and is seen as a major blow to the country's economic stability and trust in its institutions.

Japan.summary: A weak earthquake struck northern Japan on Monday, but it caused no damage and there were no reports of injuries, local media said.
More than 1,000 people were arrested and 100 companies targeted in a crackdown on organized crime groups in Japan, police said on Monday, amid growing public concern over gangster activities.
A strong earthquake struck Japan on Friday, causing widespread power outages and triggering landslides in the northern island. At least 11 people were killed and several more injured in the disaster.
